titleEn: Komet Invests in Irrigation Intelligence to Close One of Agriculture’s Biggest Data Gaps
titleZh: 科迈特投资灌溉智能，填补农业最大数据空白
summaryZh: 科迈特灌溉公司宣布成立新的应用工程与数字系统部门，并推出“经验中心”，旨在通过数据采集与分析，填补喷灌系统运行中缺乏实时、精确数据的关键空白。该投资将推动喷灌科学、工程和决策的全球进步，帮助农业应对水资源短缺和气候波动。虽然聚焦于种植业灌溉，但其数据驱动决策的思路对智慧畜牧中的精准饲喂、环境监控等场景具有借鉴意义。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了传统农业设备商向数据智能转型的典型案例，其“填补测量空白”的思路可直接启发智慧畜牧中养殖环境、动物行为等关键参数的数字化监测与闭环控制。
contentFull: By Komet Irrigation Share Komet Irrigation’s new Application Engineering & Digital Systems division and Experience Hub will generate globally relevant insights to advance pivot irrigation science, engineering, and decision-making. As global agriculture faces growing pressure to improve productivity, adapt to climate volatility, and manage increasingly scarce water resources, one critical part of the production system remains surprisingly under-measured: pivot irrigation itself. While modern agriculture benefits from sophisticated data relating to genetics, fertilizer programs, crop protection, and machinery performance, irrigation decisions continue to rely heavily on experience, visual observation, and assumptions developed over decades in the field. Komet Irrigation, the Austrian multinational and global leader in precision sprinkler and end-of-system-solutions for pivot irrigation, believes that must change. Today, the company announced a significant six-figure investment in a new Application Engineering & Digital Systems division, alongside the launch of the Komet Experience Hub, a new global capability designed to generate the data, insights, and engineering knowledge needed to advance irrigation performance worldwide. Top Articles Read More Read More Read More Read More Read More Syngenta Signs MoU to Be Strategic Partner in India’s First Open-Data Agricultural Ecosystem Annam.AI Led by Global Innovation Director, Gustavo Hossri, the new division brings together engineering, testing, diagnostics, and digital innovation with a clear objective: helping the industry better understand what happens between the sprinkler and the crop. “The future of agriculture will depend on better decisions,” said Andree Groos, CEO of Komet Irrigation. “Agriculture has become increasingly data-driven, yet irrigation remains one of the least measured variables in crop production. We believe the next generation of progress will come not only from better products, but from better information.” The investment includes the establishment of the Komet Experience Hub, an advanced research, testing, training, and demonstration environment designed to study irrigation performance in unprecedented detail. The facility will support the development of new methodologies, diagnostics, and performance metrics that help growers, dealers, OEMs, and irrigation professionals make more informed decisions. For decades, questions such as how sprinkler wear affects performance, when components should be replaced, how environmental conditions influence water delivery, and how irrigation systems can be optimized have often been answered through experience and observation. Komet’s new capability seeks to bring scientific measurement and repeatable data to these questions. “Many of the industry’s most important decisions are still made with limited measurable information,” said Hossri. “Our goal is to generate data, metrics, and insights that allow growers and partners to make decisions with greater confidence. We are moving from observation to measurement, and from assumptions to evidence.” While the Experience Hub is in Brazil, the company emphasizes that the initiative is global in both purpose and application. Brazil was selected because of its advanced agricultural sector, the availability of top educated agronomists and engineers, diverse operating environments, and relevance to some of the world’s most productive farming systems. The knowledge generated through the facility will be shared across Komet’s global network and used to support growers and partners around the world. The investment also reflects a broader evolution in Komet’s approach to innovation. Beyond developing industry-leading sprinkler technologies, the company is expanding into diagnostics, performance intelligence, digital services, and data-driven decision support. Future offerings developed through the new division will help customers better understand irrigation system performance, optimize operational efficiency, and improve long-term decision-making. Already, Experience Hub has attracted significant interest from irrigation experts, growers, OEMs, dealers, and industry partners, with some visitors spending several hours exploring the facility and its capabilities. On 26 June, Komet will officially inaugurate the Experience Hub in Brazil, bringing together industry leaders, growers, researchers, partners, government representatives, and media from around the world. “As the world works to feed a growing population while managing increasingly complex environmental challenges, water will become one of agriculture’s most strategic resources,” added Groos. “We believe the future belongs to those who understand more, so they can use resources more intelligently. This investment is about helping our industry build that future.” 0 1 5 Komet Invests in Irrigation Intelligence to Close One of Agriculture's Biggest Data Gaps Subscribe Today For Subscribe Founded in 1952 and headquartered in Lienz, Austria, Komet Irrigation is a global specialist in precision water application for mechanized irrigation. author: Matt Hopkins
contentHtml:
New Application Engineering & Digital Systems division and Experience Hub will generate globally relevant insights to advance pivot irrigation science, engineering, and decision-making.
The post Komet Invests in Irrigation Intelligence to Close One of Agriculture’s Biggest Data Gaps appeared first on Global Ag Tech Initiative.
